data.title: Healthcare Triangle Launches Major Health Advisory Platform Agreement
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Healthcare Triangle ( (HCTI) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 7, 2026, Healthcare Triangle entered into a Platform Development Agreement, effective March 31, 2026, with SecureKloud Technologies as lead contractor and Blockedge Technologies as sub-contractor to design, develop, test and deliver an integrated health advisory and care platform with document management AI tools for its commercial offerings. The project is scheduled to run 12 to 15 months, but no later than September 30, 2027, and all intellectual property, including enhancements and derivative works, will be owned exclusively by Healthcare Triangle, with SKL and Blockedge retaining no rights, commissions or royalties and the tools intended for deployment chiefly in North America and Europe, with global use allowed.

Financially, the Agreement formalizes how prior advances of about $3.48 million to SKL and $0.345 million to Blockedge will be handled, enabling Healthcare Triangle to offset monthly invoices against these advances and prohibiting further payments until the advances are fully settled. The parties capped the project budget at $3.0 million plus a $0.2 million contingency, with any cost overruns above $3.2 million requiring prior written approval, tightening cost control while securing strategic control over a core health-tech platform expected to underpin the company’s future product suite.

The most recent analyst rating on (HCTI) stock is a Hold with a $0.24 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Healthcare Triangle stock, see the HCTI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HCTI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HCTI is a Neutral.

The score is weighed down primarily by weak financial performance—deep ongoing losses, margin compression, and heavy negative free cash flow—alongside a strongly bearish price trend below all major moving averages. Corporate actions provide strategic upside via the AI acquisition, but recent convertible/ATM financing introduces dilution risk, and valuation signals are not supportive due to negative earnings.

To see Spark’s full report on HCTI stock, click here.

More about Healthcare Triangle

Healthcare Triangle, Inc. is a healthcare-focused technology company that develops commercial digital platforms and tools for health advisory and care delivery. The company, together with its subsidiaries, targets deployment of its solutions primarily in North American and European markets, while retaining the flexibility to roll out its technologies globally as part of its product portfolio.

data.title: SCWorx Appeals Nasdaq Delisting, Executes Reverse Stock Split
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from SCWorx ( (WORX) ).

On April 7, 2026, SCWorx disclosed that Nasdaq had determined its securities would be delisted from the Capital Market after the company failed to regain compliance with the exchange’s $1 minimum bid price rule, following an earlier deficiency notice on April 10, 2025 and a 180‑day extension granted on October 8, 2025. Trading in SCWorx common stock is set to be suspended as of the market open on April 14, 2026, the company has appealed the delisting decision to a Nasdaq hearings panel, and it implemented a 1‑for‑15 reverse stock split effective April 10, 2026 in an effort to restore its share price above $1 and preserve its Nasdaq listing, although there is no assurance the appeal or compliance efforts will succeed, posing potential adverse effects for shareholders.

If the reverse stock split fails to sustain SCWorx’s stock price above the $1 threshold for the required trading period and the appeal is unsuccessful, the company’s shares will be removed from Nasdaq, which could impair liquidity, reduce market visibility and weigh on investor confidence. The delisting risk underscores the company’s dependence on maintaining exchange compliance and highlights the potential for heightened volatility and constrained access to capital should its securities be forced to trade off‑exchange.

More about SCWorx

SCWorx is a publicly traded company whose common stock is listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market. The company is subject to Nasdaq’s listing standards, including the requirement that its shares maintain a minimum bid price of $1, and its investor base is therefore exposed to the regulatory and liquidity dynamics of major U.S. equity exchanges.

Average Trading Volume: 13,951,353

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $1.75M

For an in-depth examination of WORX stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.title: Contango ORE Updates Investors With New Corporate Presentation
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Contango ORE ( (CTGO) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 7, 2026, Contango ORE released a new corporate presentation outlining its portfolio of gold and silver projects and disclosing the use of non-GAAP financial measures derived partly from partner Kinross Gold’s IFRS-based reporting, which the company cannot fully reconcile to U.S. GAAP without unreasonable effort. The materials emphasize extensive caution around mineral resource and reserve estimates prepared under differing standards, highlighting that such estimates may not be economically mineable and may diverge from S-K 1300 classifications, underscoring uncertainty for investors relying on these technical and financial metrics.

The company also reiterates that the feasibility and technical reports underpinning key projects like Manh Choh, Lucky Shot, Johnson Tract and Kitsault Valley are subject to significant assumptions and regulatory differences between Canadian NI 43-101 and U.S. S-K 1300. This cautious framing signals that while Contango is advancing a diversified pipeline of mineral assets, stakeholders should recognize the substantial risks, estimation uncertainty and regulatory nuances embedded in the disclosed resource, reserve and financial information.

The most recent analyst rating on (CTGO) stock is a Hold with a $21.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Contango ORE stock, see the CTGO Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CTGO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CTGO is a Neutral.

CTGO scores in the low-50s mainly due to weak underlying profitability (no revenue and ongoing net losses) and a strongly bearish technical trend, partially offset by positive TTM cash generation and improved equity. The earnings call and recent corporate actions (merger and financing/hedge actions) add support, but leverage, cash-flow volatility, and the downtrend keep the overall score restrained.

To see Spark’s full report on CTGO stock, click here.

More about Contango ORE

Contango ORE, through Contango Silver & Gold Inc., operates in the precious metals mining and exploration sector with a focus on gold and silver projects. The company holds a 30% interest in the Peak Gold joint venture managed by Kinross Gold and is also involved in the Manh Choh, Lucky Shot, Johnson Tract and Kitsault Valley projects, with reporting aligned to both U.S. S-K 1300 and Canadian NI 43-101 frameworks.

data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Caterpillar ( (CAT) ) has shared an update.

Caterpillar Inc. announced on April 8, 2026, that long-serving Chief Financial Officer Andrew Bonfield will retire effective Oct. 1, 2026, after eight years marked by record financial performance and a reinforced operating model. He will remain CFO until May 1, 2026, then serve in an advisory role through his retirement, ensuring a gradual handover during a period of strong shareholder returns.

The board appointed company veteran Kyle Epley as CFO effective May 1, 2026, elevating him from senior vice president of Global Finance Services, where he has overseen enterprise-wide finance operations and strategy. His promotion, accompanied by higher salary, incentive targets and long‑term equity awards, signals continuity in Caterpillar’s finance leadership and is intended to maintain the company’s momentum in profitable growth and value creation for stakeholders.

Epley’s nearly three decades at Caterpillar, including roles as division CFO, corporate controller and leader of the Global Finance Services Division, provide him with broad operational and financial insight across the business. Management’s public endorsements of his institutional knowledge and track record underscore expectations that the finance organization will remain a strategic asset as the company builds on its recent record sales and revenue performance.

The most recent analyst rating on (CAT) stock is a Hold with a $845.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Caterpillar stock, see the CAT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CAT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CAT is a Outperform.

CAT scores well on fundamentals and trend: strong profitability and free cash flow, plus clear bullish technical positioning above key moving averages. The main offsets are expensive valuation (high P/E and low yield) and earnings-call risks centered on rising tariffs and margin compression despite strong backlog-driven visibility.

To see Spark’s full report on CAT stock, click here.

More about Caterpillar

Caterpillar Inc., based in Irving, Texas, is the world’s leading manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, off-highway diesel and natural gas engines, industrial gas turbines and diesel-electric locomotives. With 2025 sales and revenues of $67.6 billion, it serves global Power & Energy, Construction Industries and Resource Industries markets, supported by a large independent dealer network and financing services through Cat Financial.

The company focuses on solving customers’ toughest challenges through commercial excellence and advanced technology, leveraging a highly skilled global workforce. Its diversified segments and strong dealer presence position Caterpillar as a key player in infrastructure, resource development and energy-related capital equipment worldwide, underpinning resilient demand across economic cycles.

Hawaiian Electric ( (HE) ) has issued an update.

On April 10, 2026, Hawaiian Electric Industries and Hawaiian Electric triggered payment under settlement agreements dated November 1, 2024, resolving tort-related claims from the 2023 Maui windstorm and wildfires. This followed a December 30, 2025 judgment on subrogation claims by over 200 insurers becoming final and unappealable after all insurers dismissed their appeals with prejudice.

The companies have now authorized the first of four equal annual installments of $479 million, funded from a September 2024 equity offering and held in a special purpose vehicle until all payment conditions were satisfied. With the initial payment released and all conditions met, the settlement releases are now effective, providing greater legal certainty for the utilities and wildfire claimants and clarifying a major financial obligation over a defined multi-year period.

The latest announcement is out from Livento Group ( (LIVG) ).

On September 11, 2025, Livento Group Inc. entered into a $30,000 financing agreement with AES Capital Management, LLC, bearing annual interest of 8% and maturing on September 11, 2026. The note includes a conversion feature allowing any unpaid amounts to be converted into common shares at a 35% discount to a price derived from the lowest trading prices over three separate days, potentially leading to shareholder dilution and signaling continued dependence on external financing.

This structure may provide Livento Group with short-term liquidity while shifting some risk to equity holders through possible future stock issuance at a discounted valuation. The reliance on convertible debt could affect the company’s capital structure and share performance, particularly if market conditions or trading prices move unfavorably for existing shareholders before the note’s maturity.

data.title: Cardiol Therapeutics Sets June 24, 2026 Date for Annual General Meeting of Shareholders
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Cardiol Therapeutics ( (TSE:CRDL) ).

On April 10, 2026, Cardiol Therapeutics Inc. filed a Form 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, confirming its status as a foreign private issuer reporting under Form 40-F and providing an exhibit related to shareholder governance matters. The filing was executed by Chief Financial Officer Christopher Waddick from the company’s Oakville, Ontario headquarters, underscoring Cardiol’s ongoing compliance with cross-border disclosure requirements.

In a notice filed the same day via Canadian regulators, the company announced it will hold its annual general meeting of securityholders on June 24, 2026, with a record date of May 7, 2026 for notice and voting eligibility. Only holders of Class A common shares as of that date will be entitled to vote, and Cardiol has opted to use notice-and-access delivery and to pay for proxy material distribution to objecting beneficial owners, signaling a standardized governance process for investors ahead of the meeting.

The meeting arrangements, administered by Odyssey Trust Company as Cardiol’s agent, detail that proxy materials will not be sent directly to non-objecting beneficial owners but will be made available under notice-and-access protocols. These steps clarify the mechanics for shareholder participation, which can be relevant for institutional and retail investors assessing their ability to exercise voting rights in the upcoming governance cycle.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:CRDL) stock is a Hold with a C$2.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cardiol Therapeutics stock, see the TSE:CRDL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CRDL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CRDL is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (pre-revenue, sizable losses, and sustained cash burn), partially offset by a low-debt balance sheet. Technicals are a positive contributor with an improving uptrend and constructive momentum, while valuation remains constrained due to negative earnings and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on CRDL stock, click here.

More about Cardiol Therapeutics

Cardiol Therapeutics Inc. is a foreign private issuer listed in the United States that files its continuous disclosure reports under Form 40-F. The Canada-based company is incorporated as Cardiol Therapeutics Inc. and maintains its principal executive offices in Oakville, Ontario, reflecting its operational base in the Canadian life sciences and capital markets environment.

As a cross-border issuer, Cardiol Therapeutics is subject to both Canadian securities regulation and U.S. reporting requirements. Its Class A common shares are identified under ISIN CA14161Y2006, and it uses Odyssey Trust Company as an agent for shareholder communications and meeting administration across applicable exchanges and securities regulators.

T3 Defense ( (DFNS) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 10, 2026, Water IO Ltd., controlled by T3 Defense Inc. via Star 26 Capital, completed the sale of 100% of Zorro Net Ltd. to BiomX Inc. in exchange for 1.3 million BiomX common shares and a $1.25 million promissory note. BiomX also assumed obligations toward Zorronet’s founders, including a performance-based earnout tied to 2026 results and commitments to retain key personnel.

Following the transaction, Water IO now holds about 16.57% of BiomX’s outstanding common stock, and T3 Defense, through its 67% interest in Water IO, indirectly benefits from this strategic equity position. The deal shifts T3 Defense’s exposure from direct ownership of an AI defense technology asset to a sizable stake in BiomX, potentially reshaping its portfolio mix and influence across related technology markets.

More about T3 Defense

T3 Defense Inc., through its wholly owned subsidiary Star 26 Capital Inc., holds a controlling stake of about 67% in Water IO Ltd., an Israeli public company listed on the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange. Water IO, in turn, had fully owned Zorro Net Ltd., an Israeli artificial intelligence defense technology firm focused on computer vision and autonomous surveillance for military, homeland security and critical infrastructure clients.

data.title: Equinox Gold Posts Strong Q1 2026, Slashes Debt and Launches First Dividend
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Equinox Gold ( (TSE:EQX) ) is now available.

Equinox Gold reported strong first-quarter 2026 operating performance, with 197,628 ounces of gold produced in the three months ended March 31, 2026, including 87,402 ounces from its Canadian cornerstone mines, Greenstone and Valentine. Management indicated Canadian production is expected to be second-half weighted as ramp-up continues, supported by ongoing contributions from Nicaragua and Mesquite, positioning the portfolio for robust cash generation.

During the quarter, the company used proceeds from the sale of its Brazil operations and mine cash flow to repay $990 million of debt and on March 26, 2026 paid its inaugural dividend of $0.015 per share, signaling greater financial flexibility and a formal capital return framework. Operationally, Greenstone’s winter mining and mill throughput met or exceeded expectations, Valentine’s plant operated near or above nameplate capacity, and exploration success at targets such as the new Minotaur discovery underpinned long-term growth plans, including a proposed Phase 2 expansion at Valentine and technical work at Castle Mountain and Los Filos that could eventually add more than 450,000 ounces of annual production.

Equinox Gold also highlighted that its Canadian operations are forecast to deliver an average of 543,000 ounces of gold per year from 2026 to 2036 based on new technical reports, reinforcing its ambition to be a leading Americas-focused gold producer. The company scheduled the release of full Q1 2026 financial results for May 6, 2026, along with a conference call and its annual general meeting on May 7, 2026, events that will give investors further insight into its operating momentum, capital allocation priorities and project pipeline.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:EQX) stock is a Buy with a C$23.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Equinox Gold stock, see the TSE:EQX Stock Forecast page.

To see Spark’s full report on EQX stock, click here.

More about Equinox Gold

Equinox Gold Corp. is a Canadian mining company focused on gold production, operating a portfolio of high-quality, long-life mines and development projects across the Americas. Listed on the TSX and NYSE American under the ticker EQX, the company targets disciplined execution and organic growth to offer investors diversified exposure to the gold sector.

Founded and chaired by mining entrepreneur Ross Beaty and led by an experienced management team, Equinox Gold’s key assets include the Greenstone and Valentine mines in Canada, along with operations in Nicaragua, the United States and other parts of the Americas. The company emphasizes operational excellence, balance sheet strength and long-term value creation for shareholders.

data.title: Ferrari Completes First €250 Million Buyback Tranche and Launches Second Phase of €3.5 Billion Program
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ferrari ( (IT:RACE) ) has issued an update.

Ferrari N.V. has completed the first €250 million tranche of its multi-year share buyback program, repurchasing 850,054 common shares on Euronext Milan and the NYSE between January 5 and April 9, 2026, for a total consideration of about €250 million. As a result, as of April 9 the company held 17,494,660 treasury shares, equal to 9.02% of its issued common shares, or 9.40% including special voting shares, consolidating control and supporting capital allocation flexibility.

The company will launch a second tranche of up to €250 million from April 13 to no later than August 28, 2026, funded from available cash and partly intended to service its equity incentive plans. This tranche combines a €200 million non-discretionary buyback on Euronext Milan and a €50 million mandate on the NYSE, operating under existing shareholder authorization to repurchase up to 10% of common stock, and underscores Ferrari’s commitment to sustained shareholder returns through its €3.5 billion buyback plan running to 2030.

The most recent analyst rating on (IT:RACE) stock is a Buy with a EUR360.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ferrari stock, see the IT:RACE Stock Forecast page.

More about Ferrari

Ferrari N.V. is a leading global luxury brand centered on high-performance sports cars, racing and lifestyle products. From its base in Maranello, Italy, it designs, engineers and produces iconic luxury sports cars sold in more than 60 markets, while its Scuderia Ferrari Formula 1 team underpins the brand’s heritage of exclusivity, innovation and cutting-edge performance.

Beyond automobiles, Ferrari extends its brand into personal luxury goods, collectibles and curated experiences that reflect its elevated style and racing DNA. This diversified luxury ecosystem reinforces Ferrari’s positioning at the top end of the automotive and lifestyle sectors, supporting strong pricing power and deep customer loyalty worldwide.

J-Star Holding Co., Ltd. ( (YMAT) ) has provided an announcement.

On March 31, 2026, J-Star Holding Co., Ltd. saw Chief Executive Officer Sam Van resign, a move the company said was unrelated to any dispute over operations, policies or practices. He entered into a severance agreement providing a lump-sum payment equal to six months of salary and will remain involved as an advisor to the board and management team.

That same day, the board reappointed long-time leader Jing-Bin (Jonathan) Chiang as Chief Executive Officer while he continues as chairman, formalizing his role through a new employment agreement. The board emphasized that Chiang’s deep composites-industry experience and history with J-Star are expected to provide leadership stability and continuity as the company manages global economic uncertainty and pursues long-term growth, signaling limited governance disruption for investors and other stakeholders.

On April 9, 2026, First Real Estate Investment Trust of New Jersey, Inc. declared a second-quarter dividend of $0.10 per share for common shareholders of record at the close of business on May 29, 2026. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on June 12, 2026, reflecting the Trust’s ongoing distribution policy amid a real estate environment influenced by tenant performance, market values, and broader economic conditions.

The board emphasized that the dividend will continue to be evaluated on a quarter-by-quarter basis and that there is no assurance of future payouts or that this amount signals the level of any subsequent distributions. The announcement underscores continued uncertainty for investors, as FREIT’s results and its ability to sustain dividends remain sensitive to industry risks, financial market volatility, regulatory considerations, and its ability to maintain REIT status.
